phrasing_conventions: Let f be the function defined on R as f(x) = ...
Let (C) be the representative curve of f in an orthonormal system (O; i, j).
Let (d) be the line with equation y = ...
Deduce that f has a minimum / maximum at x = ...
Study the relative positions of (C) and (d).
Show that (C) has a point of inflection at x = ... and determine its coordinates.
The equation f(x) = k has two real roots alpha and beta such that alpha < 0 < beta.
Let A(alpha) be the area of the region bounded by (C), (d), and the y-axis.
Prove that integral of ... dx = ... + k, with k in R.
curve_study_sequence: 1. Determine the domain of definition Df.
2. Calculate the limits at the boundaries of Df (at -inf, +inf, and any excluded point).
3. Study asymptotes: identify oblique asymptote y = ax+b by computing lim [f(x)-(ax+b)], then state relative position of (C) vs (d).
4. Calculate f'(x) and verify it equals a known auxiliary function g(x) already studied in Part A.
5. Deduce the sign of f'(x) from the sign table of g, then set up the table of variations of f.
6. Calculate f''(x). Show it vanishes at a specific x0 and changes sign, concluding (C) has a point of inflection at x0.
7. Draw (d) and (C) in a named orthonormal system, labelling the minimum, the asymptote, and the inflection point.
function_types_observed: x * e^x  (most frequent — appears in ~70% of sessions)

functions_curve_study.structure: Two parts. Part A: define auxiliary function g(x) = f'(x), study g fully (limits, variations, sign table). Part B: define main function f, use g from Part A to find f', study f fully, find asymptote, inflection point, compute an integral or area bounded by (C) and (d).

complex_numbers.structure: Define points A, B, C with given affixes. Define a transformation M -> M'. Questions: exponential form of z', algebraic form of a ratio to deduce triangle nature, verify transformation formula, deduce modulus relation, find locus of M'.

probability.structure: Two parts. Part A: simple urn model, simultaneous draws, compute P(A) using combinations. Part B: compound experiment (die + urn), compute conditional probabilities using total probability formula, apply Bayes theorem. Must define random variable X and compute E(X).

space_geometry.structure: Orthonormal frame in space with specific points. Questions: find normal vector, equation of a plane, parametric equations of a line, distance from point to plane, intersection of line and plane, nature of a solid.

sequences.structure: Define sequence (u_n). Show it is arithmetic or geometric or neither. Find general term. Study monotonicity. Compute sum S_n. Study limit.
